name: QA 1614
description:
PCP QA Test No. 1614

pmlogpush testing



valgrind variant, see qa/1615 for the non-valgrind variant



check-group-include: pmlogpush

**To reproduce this test, please run:**

    build/ci/ci-run.py ubuntu2004 reproduce

statusMessage:
output mismatch (see 1614.out.bad)
39a40,74
> pmproxy failed to start on port 44322 after 20 seconds
> likely looking processes ...
> UID          PID    PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
> pmproxy logfile (/var/log/pcp/pmproxy/pmproxy.log) ...
> Log for pmproxy on runnervmw9dnm started Thu Nov 13 20:57:39 2025
> 
> pmproxy: PID = 1813246, PDU version = 2, user = pcp (997)
> pmproxy request port(s):
>   sts fd   port  family address
>   === ==== ===== ====== =======
>   ok    12       unix   /run/pcp/pmproxy.socket
>   ok    13 44322 inet   INADDR_ANY
>   ok    14 44322 ipv6   INADDR_ANY
>   ok    15 44323 inet   INADDR_ANY
>   ok    16 44323 ipv6   INADDR_ANY
> [Thu Nov 13 20:57:40] pmproxy(1813246) Info: pmproxy caught SIGTERM
> [Thu Nov 13 20:57:40] pmproxy(1813246) Info: pmproxy Shutdown
> 
> Log finished Thu Nov 13 20:57:40 2025
> --- start systemctl status for pmproxy.service ---
> x pmproxy.service - Proxy for Performance Metrics Collector Daemon
>      Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/pmproxy.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
>      Active: failed (Result: start-limit-hit) since Thu 2025-11-13 20:57:40 UTC; 19s ago
>    Duration: 1.165s
>        Docs: man:pmproxy(1)
>     Process: 1813246 ExecStart=/etc/pcp/pmproxy/rc start-systemd (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
>    Main PID: 1813246 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
>         CPU: 70ms
> --- end systemctl status for pmproxy.service ---
> --- start journalctl entries in last minute for -u pmproxy ---
> Hint: You are currently not seeing messages from other users and the system.
>       Users in groups 'adm', 'systemd-journal' can see all messages.
>       Pass -q to turn off this notice.
> No journal files were opened due to insufficient permissions.
> --- end recent journalctl entries for -u pmproxy ---
Check local PMCD is still alive ...
